=== Metadata ===
Effect version: 4.0.0-beta.27

/.src/unnecessaryFailYieldableError.ts(22,3): suggestion TS377019: This Effect.fail call uses a yieldable error type as argument. You can yield* the error directly instead. effect(unnecessaryFailYieldableError)
/.src/unnecessaryFailYieldableError.ts(23,3): suggestion TS377019: This Effect.fail call uses a yieldable error type as argument. You can yield* the error directly instead. effect(unnecessaryFailYieldableError)


==== /.src/unnecessaryFailYieldableError.ts (2 errors) ====
    /** @effect-diagnostics missingReturnYieldStar:skip-file */
    import * as Data from "effect/Data"
    import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"
    import * as Schema from "effect/Schema"
    
    class NonYieldable extends Error {
      readonly _tag = "NonYieldable"
    }
    
    class DataTaggedError extends Data.TaggedError("DataTaggedError")<{}> {}
    
    class SchemaError extends Schema.ErrorClass<SchemaError>("SchemaError")({
      _tag: Schema.tag("SchemaError")
    }) {}
    
    export const unnecessaryFailYieldableError = Effect.gen(function*() {
      // should not report
      yield* Effect.fail(new NonYieldable("non-yieldable error"))
      const err = new DataTaggedError()
      console.log(err)
      // should report
      yield* Effect.fail(new DataTaggedError())
      ~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377019: This Effect.fail call uses a yieldable error type as argument. You can yield* the error directly instead. effect(unnecessaryFailYieldableError)
      yield* Effect.fail(new SchemaError({}))
      ~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377019: This Effect.fail call uses a yieldable error type as argument. You can yield* the error directly instead. effect(unnecessaryFailYieldableError)
    })
    
    // should not report
    export const shouldNotReport = Effect.fail(new DataTaggedError())
